content.text:
All 11 tests pass. Here's a summary of what was built:

**Files created:**
- `FileRenamer.Tests.ps1` — 11 Pester tests written first (TDD red phase)
- `FileRenamer.ps1` — implementation written to make them pass (green phase)

**Three functions implemented:**

| Function | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `Get-RenamePreview` | Returns rename pairs without touching disk; marks conflicts |
| `Invoke-BatchRename` | Performs renames, respects `-WhatIf`, skips conflicts, optionally generates undo |
| `New-UndoScript` | Writes a `.ps1` that reverses each rename |

**Test coverage:**
- Correct regex substitution with capture groups
- Files not matching pattern are excluded
- Inter-rename conflict detection (two files → same name)
- Bystander collision detection (target name already exists)
- `-WhatIf` preview mode (no disk changes)
- Conflict skip during actual rename
- Result objects returned per file
- Undo script content correctness
- Undo script actually reverses renames on disk

result:
All 11 tests pass. Here's a summary of what was built:

**Files created:**
- `FileRenamer.Tests.ps1` — 11 Pester tests written first (TDD red phase)
- `FileRenamer.ps1` — implementation written to make them pass (green phase)

**Three functions implemented:**

| Function | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `Get-RenamePreview` | Returns rename pairs without touching disk; marks conflicts |
| `Invoke-BatchRename` | Performs renames, respects `-WhatIf`, skips conflicts, optionally generates undo |
| `New-UndoScript` | Writes a `.ps1` that reverses each rename |

**Test coverage:**
- Correct regex substitution with capture groups
- Files not matching pattern are excluded
- Inter-rename conflict detection (two files → same name)
- Bystander collision detection (target name already exists)
- `-WhatIf` preview mode (no disk changes)
- Conflict skip during actual rename
- Result objects returned per file
- Undo script content correctness
- Undo script actually reverses renames on disk
